channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: How to Set Up a Free Coding Agent on Your Machine in 10 Minutes
item.description: I pay for Claude Code. I use it every day, I built skills for it, and I think it's worth the money. I'm saying this upfront because this post is going to show you how to get a coding agent for...

item.title: I'm building a production toolkit for Claude's new Managed Agents API
item.description: Anthropic released the Managed Agents API on April 8 — and it's a game changer. You can now create persistent AI agents with tools, memory, and multi-step orchestration built in. But here's the...

item.title: Making Alpine.js Transitions Feel "Creamy" with Alpine Turnout 🍦
item.description: Stop Routing, Start Switching Most SPA routers are heavy, complex, and require you to define your entire site structure in a separate JavaScript file. Alpine Turnout takes a different approach. It...

item.title: 🚨 Java & JavaScript Are NOT Call by Reference (Let’s Break This Myth)
item.description: One of the most common misconceptions in programming: “Objects are passed by reference in Java or JavaScript” ❌ That’s not true. 👉 Both Java and JavaScript are call by value 🧠 Why the Confusion?...

item.title: Next.js vs Vite in 2026: What you should actually use
item.description: Next.js vs Vite in 2026: What You Should Actually Use The JavaScript ecosystem continues evolving rapidly, and by 2026, both Next.js and Vite have matured significantly. This in-depth technical...

item.title: Why I Stopped Writing (And What Happened Since)
item.description: Originally published at devopsdiary.blog. Series opener for "The Quiet Years," a retrospective on the work between August 2022 and now. The last post on this blog went up in August 2022. Three and...

item.title: Checking TLS cert expiry across your infrastructure
item.description: A cert expired on one of our staging services last month. Nobody noticed for two hours because our monitoring only checked HTTP 200s, and the TLS error happened before HTTP even got involved. The...

item.title: 3 Prototype Pollution Bugs Cursor Keeps Writing Into Your Code
item.description: TL;DR AI editors generate deep-merge and object-spread patterns vulnerable to prototype pollution Attackers inject proto properties to override Object defaults and bypass auth Use...

item.title: 🎙️ Building a Voice-Controlled AI Agent with Tool Execution
item.description: In this article, I’ll walk through how I built a voice-controlled AI agent that can understand user commands, decide what action to take, execute tools like file creation or code generation, and...

item.title: Most Startup Ideas Are Backwards — I Search for Products People Already Resent
item.description: I've never liked the usual advice about finding startup ideas. "Study market size." "Brainstorm a hundred ideas." "Find an underserved niche." It all sounds clean on paper. In practice, it's...

item.title: Best Buy’s Ultimate Upgrade Sale features deals on dozens of our favorite gadgets
item.description: If you missed out on Amazon’s recent spring sales event, Best Buy’s Ultimate Upgrade Sale presents yet another opportunity to score steep discounts on some of our favorite gadgets. The five-day...

item.title: The Senate is voting to save free IRS Direct File today
item.description: The Senate is getting ready to vote on a bill to resurrect IRS Direct File, the free tax filing service axed by the Trump administration in 2025. On Wednesday, Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-MA) will...

item.title: Most people still don’t want anything to do with robotaxis
item.description: I've been reporting on self-driving cars for over a decade, and I've seen the technology go through many ups and downs, highs and lows. But one thing has remained remarkably the same over the...

item.title: Allbirds announced a switch from shoes to AI and its stock jumped 600 percent
item.description: Allbirds had a hit a decade ago with its Wool Runner shoes, but after a $4 billion IPO in 2021, the business never turned a profit, and sales dropped nearly 50 percent between 2022 and 2025. The...

item.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: the next generation of expressive AI speech
item.description: Gemini logo next to the text "3.1 Flash TTS", all over colored dots

item.title: Allbirds Is Pivoting to AI Compute. Sure, Why Not
item.description: Once a $4 billion apparel juggernaut, Allbirds will rebrand as NewBird AI, a “GPU-as-a-Service” company. Hey, if you can't beat ’em, join ’em.

item.title: After sale of its shoe business, Allbirds pivots to AI
item.description: Allbirds is ditching wool sneakers for AI servers, rebranding as NewBird AI after locking in a $50M convertible financing facility.

item.title: The perfect successor to Lost has been hiding from me for years
item.description: Ever since Jack decided to stay behind in 2010, I've been searching for something to give me the same feeling that Lost did. I crave a big mystery with a huge cast and more secrets than I can...

item.title: Walmart is updating its 4K streaming box with Gemini and Matter support
item.description: It hasn't been officially announced, but Walmart's website now has a listing (you may need to set your location to Ventura, California) for an updated version of its budget-friendly Onn 4K Pro...

item.title: Reid Hoffman weighs in on the ‘tokenmaxxing’ debate
item.description: Reid Hoffman says tracking AI token use can gauge adoption, but cautions it should be paired with context and not treated as a direct productivity metric.

item.title: Adobe’s new Firefly AI assistant can use Creative Cloud apps to complete tasks
item.description: Adobe says the assistant can work across apps like Firefly, Photoshop, Premiere, Lightroom, Express, Illustrator and its other apps to do tasks for you.

item.title: Gitar, a startup that uses agents to secure code, emerges from stealth with $9 million
item.description: The company uses AI to review code that, more often than not these days, has also been generated by AI.

item.title: This startup is betting tokenmaxxing will create the next compute giant
item.description: Parasail raised $32 million in a Series A, signaling a fractured future of models and compute.

item.title: More phone cameras should come with telephoto lenses
item.description: Upgrading your phone with a camera grip attachment is one thing. But doll-sized telephoto lenses that you mount on top of the rear camera? C'mon. I wrote the Vivo X300 off as a gimmick, a funny...

item.title: Adobe embraces conversational AI editing, marking a ‘fundamental shift’ in creative work
item.description: Adobe is fully embracing AI tools that enable creators to edit their work using descriptive prompts, instead of manually using specific Creative Cloud apps. The software giant's new Firefly AI...

item.title: Amazon’s new Fire TV Stick HD is its ‘slimmest ever’
item.description: Amazon has taken the wraps off a new Fire TV Stick HD that it says is its slimmest yet. At $34.99, the device offers a design that Amazon says is 30 percent thinner than the previous generation,...

item.title: Actively Exploited nginx-ui Flaw (CVE-2026-33032) Enables Full Nginx Server Takeover
item.description: A recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting nginx-ui, an open-source, web-based Nginx management tool, has come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability in question is...

item.title: Nothing makes it easy to share files between any Android phone and a Mac
item.description: I test Android phones for a living, but I write about them using a company-supplied MacBook Air. Both platforms are great in their own right, but they're not so great at talking to one another. On...

item.title: April Patch Tuesday Fixes Critical Flaws Across SAP, Adobe, Microsoft, Fortinet, and More
item.description: A number of critical vulnerabilities impacting products from Adobe, Fortinet, Microsoft, and SAP have taken center stage in April's Patch Tuesday releases. Topping the list is an SQL injection...

item.title: The Download: NASA’s nuclear spacecraft and unveiling our AI 10
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. NASA is building the first nuclear reactor-powered...
